package: Goal 16: Paper-Style Interacting Bridge Code Theorem

scope.paper_theorem_family: m encoded mouths, a permutation mouth map, right stabilizer blocks of distance d, and an arbitrary simple graph-CZ interaction on the right logical mouths
scope.executable_bound: all simple graphs and all mouth permutations for m<=3 with dense channel checks

core_claim: Pairwise inter-bridge MI is not an arbitrary-graph reader, but state-derived logical Pauli-correlation tomography recovers the graph-CZ interaction, dressed observer algebra, and exact inverse-interaction bridge transfer while low-order entropy through distance remains blind to the mouth map.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: Full encoded-block mutual information identifies the mouth permutation pi.
The unique right-logical-Z dressing that makes X_Li Xbar_pi(i) a stabilizer recovers the neighbor set of pi(i) in the arbitrary graph G.
Conjugating the right logical Pauli algebra by the recovered CZ_G reconstructs the dressed observer algebra.
For fixed G, labeled physical entropy probes through order d do not determine the mouth permutation.
Removing the recovered interaction and routing by the recovered permutation restores exact bridge transfer.
The same static state signature is compatible with opposite north/south screen-channel completions.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: all simple graphs and all mouth permutations for m<=3
m=3 path graph with twisted pairing

diagnostic_hierarchy.low_order_entropy_through_distance: blind to the mouth map for fixed interaction graph
diagnostic_hierarchy.pairwise_inter_bridge_mi: detects non-product correlations but fails as an arbitrary-graph reader
diagnostic_hierarchy.dressed_observer_algebra: recovered from the inferred interaction graph
diagnostic_hierarchy.static_state_data: insufficient for screen recovery transitions without dynamics

proof_obligations.claim: Regression check for Goal 15 plus Goal 16 interacting bridge certificates.
Validate the machine-readable Goal 16 certificate index.

known_vs_new.known_derived: Stabilizer row-space membership, logical CZ conjugation, stabilizer entropy, Bell transfer, and finite Choi/Kraus channel checks.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: A finite theorem-family benchmark separating weak inter-bridge MI from complete state-derived Pauli-correlation recovery of arbitrary graph-CZ bridge interactions.

limitations: Finite stabilizer/QEC theorem package only. The executable atlas is bounded to m<=3 dense checks; the arbitrary-m proof is the Pauli-correlation stabilizer argument. This is not a continuum ER=EPR, de Sitter, dS/CFT, or many-body chaos theorem.
